task_completion.description: Section and subsection headings are formatted
task_completion.visual_evidence: Check if the 16 main sections (e.g., '1. Identification', '2. Hazard(s) Identification') and subsections (e.g., 'Control Parameters') are visually styled as headings (e.g., larger font size, bolded) in Calligra Words.

task_completion.description: Signal word is formatted and hazard codes are bolded
task_completion.visual_evidence: Check if the signal word 'DANGER' has distinct formatting (e.g., capitalized, bold, centered) and the hazard codes ('H225', 'H319', 'H336') are bolded in the text.

task_completion.description: Tables are created for structured data
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for tabular structures (rows and columns) organizing data within the document, replacing previously unformatted text.

task_completion.description: A table of contents is present
task_completion.visual_evidence: Check for a generated Table of Contents block, typically located at the beginning of the document, listing the sections.
Check if body paragraphs (e.g., 'Keep away from heat/sparks/open flames') have consistent alignment applied (such as justified text or specific indentations) compared to raw text.
Check the Calligra Words window title bar for the absence of an unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk next to the filename), or look for a save action being performed.

integrity.description: Agent used Calligra Words for formatting
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the formatting actions were performed using the Calligra Words GUI, rather than using terminal scripts, pandoc, or another word processor.

integrity.description: Chemical data and values were not altered
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that specific chemical values (e.g., '67-63-0', '400 ppm', '0.786', '82.6') are still present in the document and have not been modified or deleted.

integrity.description: Agent applied formatting rather than pasting a completed document
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of the agent interacting with formatting tools (styles, bold button, table insertion) rather than simply pasting a pre-formatted SDS from an external source.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The document should contain 16 H1 sections (from '1. Identification' to '16. Other Information') and specific H2 subsections ('Control Parameters', 'Exposure Controls', 'DOT (US)'). The signal word is 'DANGER'. Hazard codes to be bolded are 'H225', 'H319', 'H336'. Body text samples include 'Keep away from heat/sparks/open flames'. Chemical data that must remain unaltered includes '67-63-0', '82.6', '11.7', '5045', '1219', '400 ppm', '0.786', '399', and 'Packing group: II'.
